title: World reacts to US, Israel attack on Iran, Tehran retaliation - Al Jazeera
summary: Global leaders are closely monitoring the escalating conflict in the Middle East following reported strikes by the US and Israel against Iran, which prompted immediate retaliatory actions from Tehran. The international community is expressing deep concern over the potential for wider regional destabilization as tensions continue to mount.

title: World News in Brief: Rafah crossing reopens, gender inequality worsens global water crisis, rights defenders in Colombia - UN News
summary: The Rafah crossing has reportedly reopened, offering a critical lifeline for humanitarian aid and movement in the region. Meanwhile, a new report highlights how gender inequality is exacerbating the global water crisis, disproportionately affecting women and girls, while human rights defenders in Colombia continue to face significant challenges.

title: From Japan to Israel, elections are reshaping global politics - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
summary: Recent electoral outcomes across various nations, from Japan to Israel, are significantly altering the international political landscape. These shifts reflect evolving public sentiments and are poised to influence foreign policy and regional alliances in the coming years.

summary: A recent analysis from the World Economic Forum suggests that global cooperation is undergoing a transformation rather than a decline. This perspective indicates a shift in how nations engage on international issues, adapting to new geopolitical realities and challenges.

title: 9 Current Conflicts in the World Today and Their Effects on Global Hunger - World Food Program USA
summary: Ongoing conflicts worldwide are significantly exacerbating the global hunger crisis, displacing populations and disrupting food supply chains. The World Food Program highlights how these protracted disputes are pushing millions into severe food insecurity, demanding urgent international attention and intervention.

title: India-Nepal Relations | Current Affairs - Vision IAS
summary: India and Nepal share deep historical and cultural ties, forming a crucial bilateral relationship in South Asia. Recent discussions have focused on strengthening economic cooperation and resolving border disputes to foster regional stability.

title: India proposes new rules to regulate news and political posts on social media - BBC
summary: The Indian government has unveiled new regulations aimed at overseeing news and political content shared on social media platforms. These proposed rules seek to curb misinformation and enhance accountability, sparking debates about censorship and digital freedoms.

title: MeitY secretary says every social media user posting on current affairs can come under I&B oversight - ThePrint
summary: A senior official from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology indicated that all social media users discussing current affairs could fall under the purview of the Information and Broadcasting Ministry. title: Communalism in India, Meaning, Causes, Effects, UPSC Notes - Vajiram & Ravi
summary: Communalism remains a significant challenge in India, characterized by the politicization of religious identity and inter-group tensions. Understanding its historical roots and socio-economic impacts is crucial for fostering national cohesion and secular values.

title: PM Modi Becomes India’s Longest-Serving Head of Government - GK Today
summary: Prime Minister Modi has achieved a historic milestone, becoming India's longest-serving head of government. This tenure reflects a significant period of political stability and policy implementation under his leadership.

title: OECD Says 60% of Chinese Gains in Market Share Driven by Subsidies - WSJ
summary: A recent OECD report indicates that a significant portion of China's increased global market share, specifically 60%, is attributable to government subsidies. This finding raises concerns about fair competition and could intensify international trade discussions.

title: After Taiwan, South Korea overtakes India to become world’s sixth largest stock market; here’s why - The Times of India
summary: South Korea has ascended to become the world's sixth-largest stock market, surpassing India after previously overtaking Taiwan. This shift highlights the growing economic influence and investor confidence in South Korea's financial landscape.

summary: Major U.S. stock index futures, including the S&P 500 and Dow Jones, are experiencing declines today following President Trump's recent remarks on Iran negotiations. This statement has injected fresh geopolitical uncertainty, leading to a cautious market sentiment.
